name: rule_8c_no_customer_artifact
description: Pins the PSC-as-evidence fix (KERNEL_VERSION 0.8.0). A program-specific control (PSC) mapping in the SDA Controls tab establishes that a requirement is IN SCOPE for the program — it is NOT a record that the requirement was implemented. Pre-0.8.0, Rule 8c short-circuited to COMPLIANT by restating the verified SDA Controls shall-statement as the narrative, i.e. it used the program-control requirement text as proof of compliance even with zero customer-side artifacts (it only flipped needs_review=True). That was the 'program-specific controls used as evidence' bug. The fix (assessor.py:_finalize_sda_gap_decision) demotes the mapping: when the CCI is on the whitelist at supersession._SSAA_TO_SDA_MAPPINGS (3 CCIs as of 2026-06-06) but NO customer-side artifact is tagged, the deterministic verdict is Non-Compliant — the requirement is in-scope-but-undemonstrated and a POA&M is opened. No LLM call is made (Rule 8c fires before step 1.65 no-evidence and step 2 LLM). source stays 'rule-8c' so VerdictSource routing is unchanged; needs_review is False so the NC flows to the workbook/POAM exports like any other evidence-gap finding. CCI-001485 (AU-2 → SDA Req #29) is the smallest of the three mapped CCIs. tagged_evidence is whitespace-only so the legacy raw-string path trips no_sda_artifact=True; an EvidenceBlock with has_artifacts=False would do the same via the preferred path. Empty llm_stub_proposals confirms the LLM is never called. The narrative names the SDA Req as in-scope without quoting the shall-statement (rule #11.2) and contains 'POA&M'/'presumed not satisfied' so the gap classifier fires.

ccis_row.guidance: The information system generates audit records for the events defined in AU-2 d with the content defined in AU-3.
ccis_row.procedures: Examine audit configuration; confirm the system generates the required audit records.
